name: Toni Preckwinkle
bio: Toni Preckwinkle is the incumbent Cook County Board President, first elected in 2010, and chair of the Cook County Democratic Party. She previously served as a Chicago alderperson representing the 4th Ward for nearly two decades. (Source: Cook County official website; WTTW Voter Guide 2026)
prior_office: Cook County Board President (2010–present); Chicago Alderman (4th Ward, 1991–2010)

jacket.note: County race — ILSBE filing, not FEC.

donors.name: Jay Robert Pritzker Revocable Trust (Gov. Pritzker)
Chicago Regional Council of Carpenters PAC

red_flags.label: Connected to Ald. Burke Federal Corruption Case
red_flags.detail: Preckwinkle returned over $100,000 in fundraising linked to indicted Chicago Alderman Edward Burke following his 2019 federal corruption charges — a documented fact per Chicago Tribune reporting. Reports have also alleged she hired Burke's son as a six-figure county employee, though this has not been independently confirmed. Preckwinkle was not charged in connection with the Burke case and has denied any quid pro quo arrangement.

red_flags.label: Soda Tax Backlash — Revenue Motive Admitted
red_flags.detail: Preckwinkle championed a controversial penny-per-ounce soda tax in 2017, which reached 87% disapproval in polls and was repealed. She later admitted the primary motivation was revenue, not public health.

red_flags.label: Tyler Technologies contract scandal — $265M+ in contracts and $122M in borrowing
red_flags.detail: Tyler Technologies contract scandal — $265M+ in contracts and $122M in borrowing by other taxing bodies; delayed 2024 tax bills; Preckwinkle acknowledged project was 'bumpy'

red_flags.label: Machine politician ties — chairs Cook County Democratic Party, uses endorsement
red_flags.detail: Machine politician ties — chairs Cook County Democratic Party, uses endorsement power as de facto patronage (per critics)

red_flags.label: 2026: Supported ICE data-sharing contract renewal — contradicts stated sanctuary
red_flags.detail: 2026: Supported ICE data-sharing contract renewal — contradicts stated sanctuary city values; several board commissioners abstained in protest

red_flags.label: Sales tax hike (2015) after campaigning against it in 2010 — documented broken c
red_flags.detail: Sales tax hike (2015) after campaigning against it in 2010 — documented broken campaign promise

red_flags.label: Donor base dominated by public-sector unions (SEIU, AFSCME, Operating Engineers,
red_flags.detail: Donor base dominated by public-sector unions (SEIU, AFSCME, Operating Engineers, Carpenters) — raises potential conflicts with county labor negotiations

red_flags.label: Tyler Technologies contract scandal — $265M+ in contracts and $122M in borrowing by other taxing bodies; delayed 2024 tax bills; Preckwinkle acknowledged project was 'bumpy'
Machine politician ties — chairs Cook County Democratic Party, uses endorsement power as de facto patronage (per critics)
2026: Supported ICE data-sharing contract renewal — contradicts stated sanctuary city values; several board commissioners abstained in protest
Sales tax hike (2015) after campaigning against it in 2010 — documented broken campaign promise
Donor base dominated by public-sector unions (SEIU, AFSCME, Operating Engineers, Carpenters) — raises potential conflicts with county labor negotiations

key_votes.bill: 2015: Pushed through Cook County sales tax increase (1% → restore Stroger-era ta
key_votes.summary: 2015: Pushed through Cook County sales tax increase (1% → restore Stroger-era tax) to fund pensions — later drew attack ads citing 2010 campaign promise not to raise it

key_votes.bill: 2017: Led sweetened beverage tax (soda tax) — repealed after 4 months following
key_votes.summary: 2017: Led sweetened beverage tax (soda tax) — repealed after 4 months following industry-funded backlash campaign

key_votes.bill: 2020-2024: Oversaw Tyler Technologies contract for property tax system upgrade —
key_votes.summary: 2020-2024: Oversaw Tyler Technologies contract for property tax system upgrade — cost overruns totaling $265M+ and delayed tax bills in 2024, acknowledged as troubled

key_votes.bill: 2024: Cook County board approved sunsetting of Sheriff's electronic monitoring p
key_votes.summary: 2024: Cook County board approved sunsetting of Sheriff's electronic monitoring program, transferring oversight to Chief Judge

key_votes.bill: 2025-2026: Supported renewal of data contract with company providing information
key_votes.summary: 2025-2026: Supported renewal of data contract with company providing information to ICE and federal agencies — drew criticism from immigrant advocates

key_votes.bill: 16 years: Balanced budgets, 4 bond rating upgrades, no property tax/fee increase
key_votes.summary: 16 years: Balanced budgets, 4 bond rating upgrades, no property tax/fee increases in recent years, pension funding improved from near-insolvency to 66% funded

key_votes.bill: Criminal justice reform champion — reduced pretrial detention, led Cook County H
key_votes.summary: Criminal justice reform champion — reduced pretrial detention, led Cook County Health expansion, supported Kim Foxx as State's Attorney

endorsements.name: South and West Side clergy (dozens, per campaign rally Feb 5, 2026)
SEIU Illinois State Council (major donor and consistent backer)
Illinois Congressional Delegation (state's top leaders per Tribune March 11, 2026)

career_history.highlight: Longest-serving County Board President; oversaw $6B+ budget

policy_platform.position: Supported bail reform and reducing pre-trial detention. Has called for accountability at Cook County Jail — complicated by reliance on Sheriff Dart.
Led Cook County Health expansion; championed mental health services as alternative to incarceration.
Passed controversial soda tax (2016, later repealed). Supports progressive revenue tools.

social_pulse.summary: Establishment Democrat with mixed sentiment. Respected for longevity and institutional competence; criticized by progressives for relationships with machine figures like Dart. High name recognition.

trust_indicators.label: Soda tax controversy (2016) — passed then repealed
No personal ethics violations on record
Cook County Democratic Party Chair
trust_indicators.value: Dual role raises questions about conflicts of interest

news_hits.headline: Cook County Board president race hinges on whether voters want steady hand or new course
news_hits.summary: The Chicago Tribune's pre-primary analysis examines the race between incumbent Preckwinkle and challenger Ald. Brendan Reilly (42nd Ward), framing it as an establishment vs. modernization contest. Reilly has raised competitively and made the Tyler Technologies contract cost overruns a central attack.

news_hits.headline: Preckwinkle seeks fifth term as Cook County Board President, talks political future
news_hits.summary: WGN-TV reports Preckwinkle is seeking an unprecedented fifth term and faces a serious primary challenge from Chicago Ald. Brendan Reilly, who has made the county's property tax system modernization failures and Preckwinkle's age (she is 79) key issues in the race.

news_hits.headline: Here are 8 races to watch in the 2026 Illinois primary election
news_hits.title: Here are 8 races to watch in the 2026 Illinois primary election
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago's final pre-primary roundup includes the Cook County Board President race among 8 must-watch contests. Notes Preckwinkle is seeking a fifth term pledging improved healthcare access, criminal justice reform, and youth employment, against challenger Ald. Brendan Reilly.

news_hits.title: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.summary: WBEZ comprehensive March 17 primary preview covers all key races including Cook County Board President (Preckwinkle vs. Reilly), Cook County Assessor (Kaegi vs. Hynes), U.S. Senate, governor, and comptroller.

news_hits.title: What elections are coming up in Illinois? Here's what's ahead in the 2026 primary
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago overview of key 2026 primary races, covering Preckwinkle vs. Reilly for Cook County Board President and Kaegi vs. Hynes for Assessor among the notable local contests.

news_hits.title: On final weekend before Illinois primary, candidates try to keep it local despite flood of out-of-state money
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune's final-weekend dispatch covers candidates canvassing the South Side Irish Parade, Black churches, and local rallies on the eve of the March 17 primary. Preckwinkle, Reilly, Krishnamoorthi, Kelly, and Stratton all named; the piece emphasizes how tens of millions in out-of-state PAC money (pro-Israel AIPAC, crypto, AI interests) have nationalized what were traditionally local races.

news_hits.title: Deadbeat Chicago developer owes another $1 million in back rent to the CTA
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times watchdog report on developer Scott Goodman's $1M in unpaid CTA rent also reveals he made a $1,032 campaign contribution to Preckwinkle this month, as she is in a tight primary against Ald. Reilly. Raises questions about the campaign's donor vetting.

news_hits.title: A historic Senate opening meets a divided Illinois Democratic Party
news_hits.summary: Politico's pre-primary deep-dive notes Preckwinkle lent advisers from her political operation to Krishnamoorthi's Senate campaign — a move read by insiders as a subtle vote of confidence for Krishnamoorthi over Stratton.

news_hits.title: Cold weather to greet Illinois voters Tuesday as primary features open US Senate, crowded House races
news_hits.summary: Tribune election-eve dispatch confirms cold weather will greet Illinois primary voters on March 17. Covers all major races: open U.S. Senate (Krishnamoorthi/Kelly/Stratton), Cook County Board President (Preckwinkle vs. Reilly), and governor. Pritzker and Stratton campaign signs featured in photo from final day of early voting. Notes early voting trends ahead of Election Day turnout.

news_hits.title: Veteran incumbent Toni Preckwinkle looks to fend off rival in Democratic primary
news_hits.summary: Election Day coverage of the Cook County Board President Democratic primary. Preckwinkle faces Ald. Brendan Reilly in a heated race over property tax system delays, budget management, and Cook County's fiscal future. Running for her fifth four-year term — which would equal George Dunne's tenure as the board's longest-serving leader.

news_hits.title: 2026 primary day election results: Cook County president
news_hits.summary: Four-term incumbent Cook County Board President Toni Preckwinkle faces challenger Ald. Brendan Reilly in today's Democratic primary for a fifth term.

news_hits.title: Election Night results: Preckwinkle leads Reilly 67.8%-32.2% with majority counted
news_hits.summary: As of 9:11 PM ET with the majority of Cook County votes counted, Toni Preckwinkle is winning the Democratic primary for Cook County Board President with 67.8% (359,432 votes) to challenger Ald. Brendan Reilly's 32.2% (170,800 votes). Preckwinkle is on track to win her fifth four-year term, which would tie George Dunne as the board's longest-serving president.

news_hits.title: Pat Hynes defeats Assessor Fritz Kaegi, as Cook County Board President Toni Preckwinkle wins Democratic primary
news_hits.summary: Cook County Assessor Fritz Kaegi conceded to challenger Pat Hynes in the race for the Democratic nomination, while Cook County Board President Toni Preckwinkle declared victory against challenger Ald. Brendan Reilly in Tuesday's primary.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton, Daniel Biss, Darren Bailey among winners in 2026 Illinois primary elections
news_hits.summary: Chicago Sun-Times live election results coverage includes Preckwinkle's primary victory over Ald. Reilly, with staff celebrating at Little Black Pearl in Chicago.

news_hits.title: Preckwinkle to continue as Cook County Board President
news_hits.summary: Axios Chicago post-primary wrap confirms Cook County Board President Toni Preckwinkle defeated challenger Ald. Brendan Reilly, securing her fifth term and tying George Dunne as the longest-serving Cook County Board president.

news_hits.title: Toni Preckwinkle wins primary race for Cook County Board president, AP projects
news_hits.summary: Fox 32 Chicago reports AP has projected Toni Preckwinkle wins the Democratic nomination for Cook County Board president, defeating Ald. Brendan Reilly. Preckwinkle will be the prohibitive favorite in the November general election in heavily Democratic Cook County.

news_hits.title: Two incumbents fall, appointees hold on in Cook County Board races
news_hits.summary: Cook County primary roundup: Preckwinkle won re-election as county board president while two incumbent county commissioners lost their seats to challengers.

news_hits.title: Preckwinkle Wins Democratic Nomination for Cook County Board President
news_hits.summary: WTTW reports incumbent Cook County Board President Toni Preckwinkle declared victory in the March 17, 2026 Democratic primary election at a campaign event at Little Black Pearl in Kenwood. Preckwinkle is now the presumptive fifth-term Cook County Board President with no Republican challenger filed for November.
